title: Secondary mode device software access for primary mode device users
abstract: Access to functionality of a software service is enabled at a first device associated with a user based on the first device being in a primary mode. A connection may thereafter be established between the software service and a second device associated with the same user. In response to that connection, access to a subset of the functionality of the software service is limited at the second device based on the second device being in a secondary mode determined based on the second device. Because the first device and the second device are used by the same user, the software service does not represent those devices as separate users of the software service, but rather identifies only the first device, as the primary mode device, in connection with the user to other users of the software service.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method, comprising: enabling access to functionality of a software service at a first device associated with a user based on the first device being in a primary mode; and responsive to a connection established between the software service and a second device associated with the user, limiting access to a subset of the functionality of the software service at the second device based on the second device being in a secondary mode, wherein the secondary mode is determined based on the second device. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the secondary mode is one of a plurality of secondary modes, and wherein a subset of the functionality of the software service accessible at the second device is different for each of the plurality of secondary modes. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the plurality of secondary modes includes a companion mode and a view-only mode, and wherein a first subset of the functionality of the software service accessible at the second device when the second device is in the companion mode is different from a second subset of the functionality of the software service accessible at the second device when the second device is in the view-only mode. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the software service is conferencing software and the first subset of the functionality of the software service limits the second device in the companion mode to a limited subset of controls for the conferencing software and to digital whiteboarding functionality of the conferencing software. 5. The method of claim 3, wherein the software service is conferencing software and the second subset of the functionality of the software service limits the second device in the view-only mode to outputting video streams presented by the conferencing software. 6. The method of claim 3, comprising: determining whether to connect the second device to the software service in the companion mode or the view-only mode based on one or more of an input component of the second device, an output component of the second device, or a device type of the second device. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the second device is connected to the software service in the companion mode when the second device is determined to include a hardware -32- 

encoder, and wherein the second device is connected to the software service in the view-only mode when the second device is determined to omit a hardware encoder. 8. The method of any of claims 1 through 7, comprising: prompting the user to establish the connection between the software service and the second device responsive to an authentication of the user at the second device. 9. The method of any of claims 1 through 7, comprising: establishing the connection between the software service and the second device responsive to a request from the user. 10. The method of any of claims 1 through 7, comprising: changing the first device to the secondary mode and the second device to the primary mode to enable access to the functionality of the software service at the second device and to limit access to the subset of the functionality of the software service at the first device. 11. An apparatus, comprising: a memory; and a processor configured to execute instructions stored in the memory to: enable access to functionality of software service at a first device associated with a user based on the first device being in a primary mode; and responsive to a connection established between the software service and a second device associated with the user, limit access to a subset of the functionality of the software service at the second device based on the second device being in a secondary mode, wherein the secondary mode is determined based on the second device. 12. The apparatus of claim 11, wherein the secondary mode is one of a plurality of secondary modes, and wherein a subset of the functionality of the software service accessible at the second device is different for each of the plurality of secondary modes. 13. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the plurality of secondary modes includes a companion mode and a view-only mode. 14. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the secondary mode is the companion mode, and wherein a third device associated with the user is connected to the software service in the -33- 

view-only mode. 15. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the processor is configured to execute the instructions to: designate the second device in the companion mode or the view-only mode based on one or more capabilities determined for the second device. 16. The apparatus of any of claims 11 through 15, wherein the processor is configured to execute the instructions to: change the second device from the secondary mode to the primary mode based on a disconnection of the first device from the software service. 17. A non-transitory computer readable medium storing instructions operable to cause one or more processors to perform operations comprising: enabling access to functionality of software service at a first device associated with a user based on the first device being in a primary mode; and responsive to a connection established between the software service and a second device associated with the user, limiting access to a subset of the functionality of the software service at the second device based on the second device being in a secondary mode, wherein the secondary mode is determined based on the second device. 18. The non-transitory computer readable medium of claim 17, wherein the secondary mode is one of a plurality of secondary modes, and wherein a subset of the functionality of the software service accessible at the second device is different for each of the plurality of secondary modes. 19. The non-transitory computer readable medium of claim 18, wherein the plurality of secondary modes includes a companion mode and a view-only mode. 20. The non-transitory computer readable medium of claim 19, wherein the software service is conferencing software, the subset of the functionality of the software service limits the second device in the companion mode to a limited subset of controls for the conferencing software and to digital whiteboarding functionality of the conferencing software, and the subset of the functionality of the software service limits the second device in the view-only mode to outputting video streams presented by the conferencing software.
